title: innerHTML DOM XSS
description: Untrusted user input flows into innerHTML, outerHTML, insertAdjacentHTML, or jQuery HTML methods, allowing arbitrary script execution. Sanitize with DOMPurify before any DOM insertion.

title: document.write DOM XSS
description: Untrusted user input flows into document.write() or document.writeln(), allowing arbitrary script injection and document structure alteration. Refactor to use safe DOM manipulation methods instead.

title: javascript: URI XSS
description: Dynamic values are concatenated into a 'javascript:' URI scheme assigned to href or window.open. Clicking such a link executes arbitrary JavaScript in the page's context. Use event handlers instead.

title: React dangerouslySetInnerHTML XSS
description: Untrusted user input flows into dangerouslySetInnerHTML or a ref's innerHTML, bypassing React's built-in escaping and allowing script injection. Sanitize with DOMPurify before rendering.

title: React href javascript: URI XSS
description: Untrusted user data flows into an href attribute in a React component. A 'javascript:' URI will execute arbitrary JavaScript when clicked. Validate that the URL starts with 'https://', 'http://', or '/'.

title: Next.js searchParams dangerouslySetInnerHTML XSS
description: URL parameters flow into dangerouslySetInnerHTML without sanitization in a Next.js page, causing XSS during SSR or on the client. Sanitize with DOMPurify before rendering.

title: Angular ElementRef innerHTML XSS
description: User input flows into ElementRef.nativeElement.innerHTML or Renderer2.setProperty('innerHTML'), bypassing Angular's DomSanitizer. Sanitize untrusted data with DOMPurify before DOM insertion.

title: Angular bypassSecurityTrust XSS
description: User input flows into DomSanitizer.bypassSecurityTrust*() methods which explicitly disable Angular's built-in XSS protection. Sanitize input with DOMPurify before bypassing the sanitizer.

title: Angular @HostBinding innerHTML XSS Risk
description: @HostBinding bound to innerHTML or outerHTML can introduce XSS if explicitly trusted HTML is passed to it. Verify that the bound property value is strictly controlled and never user-supplied.

title: Angular Pipe bypassSecurityTrustHtml XSS
description: A custom Pipe calls bypassSecurityTrustHtml() directly, creating a blanket sanitization bypass for any value piped through it. Use DomSanitizer.sanitize() or DOMPurify instead.

title: Angular DomSanitizer SecurityContext.NONE XSS
description: User input flows into DomSanitizer.sanitize() with SecurityContext.NONE (value 0), which performs no sanitization and returns the raw string. Use SecurityContext.HTML for HTML content.

title: Vue domProps.innerHTML XSS
description: Untrusted data flows into domProps.innerHTML or a template ref's innerHTML in a Vue component. Vue does not escape HTML inserted this way. Sanitize with DOMPurify before rendering.

title: Nuxt useHead XSS via SSR Head Injection
description: User input flows into useHead() or useHeadSafe() composables, injecting HTML into SSR-rendered <head> tags via script.innerHTML, meta content, or link href. Strictly validate and encode all user-controlled values.

title: Vue v-bind:href javascript: URI XSS
description: Dynamic binding of a 'javascript:' URI to href in a Vue template allows Cross-Site Scripting. Use Vue event handlers (e.g., @click) instead of javascript URIs.
good_examples:
<!-- ✅ safe -->
<template>
  <button @click="handleAction(userAction)">Click me</button>
</template>

title: Svelte {@html} from URL/Location XSS
description: URL or location data flows directly into '{@html}', bypassing Svelte's auto-escaping. All data rendered via {@html} must be sanitized before use.
bad_examples:
<!-- ❌ unsafe Svelte component -->
<script>
  import { page } from '$app/stores';
</script>
{@html $page.url.searchParams.get('content')}
good_examples:
<!-- ✅ safe -->
<script>
  import { page } from '$app/stores';
  import DOMPurify from 'dompurify';
  $: safeContent = DOMPurify.sanitize($page.url.searchParams.get('content') ?? '');
</script>
{@html safeContent}

title: SvelteKit load() HTML Key XSS
description: Untrusted data from cookies, URL params, or request headers is returned unsanitized from a load() function under an HTML-like key. If rendered via {@html} in the template, this leads to XSS. Sanitize values before returning from load().

title: eval() / new Function() Code Injection
description: Untrusted user input flows into eval(), new Function(), or string-based setTimeout()/setInterval(), allowing arbitrary JavaScript execution. Use safer alternatives that do not execute strings as code.

title: document.domain Weakens Same-Origin Policy
description: Assigning to document.domain relaxes the Same-Origin Policy and allows cross-subdomain DOM access. Use postMessage for cross-origin communication instead.
good_examples:
// ✅ safe - use postMessage for cross-frame communication
window.parent.postMessage({ type: 'data', payload }, 'https://example.com');

title: location.hash to Script/iframe src Injection
description: location.hash flows into a script src, iframe src, or link href. Hash fragments are fully attacker-controlled and invisible to server-side defenses. Validate against a strict allowlist before using hash values for resource loading.

title: Service Worker Hijacking via User-Controlled URL
description: Untrusted data flows into serviceWorker.register() as the scriptURL. A compromised service worker intercepts all network requests and persists across reloads. Hardcode the service worker URL or validate against an allowlist.
good_examples:
// ✅ safe - hardcoded URL
navigator.serviceWorker.register('/sw.js');

title: importScripts() Code Execution in Web Worker
description: Untrusted data flows into importScripts() inside a Web Worker. importScripts() fetches and executes scripts synchronously, potentially bypassing CSP. Hardcode script URLs or validate against a strict allowlist.

title: document.execCommand insertHTML XSS
description: Untrusted data flows into document.execCommand('insertHTML' or 'createLink'), injecting HTML into a contenteditable element and potentially bypassing Trusted Types. Sanitize input with DOMPurify before calling these commands.

title: Next.js Server Action Missing Auth Check
description: A Next.js Server Action performs database operations without an authentication or session check. Attackers can invoke this endpoint directly to bypass UI restrictions. Add auth/session validation before any DB operations.

title: Next.js <Image> SSRF via Unvalidated src
description: Untrusted user input flows into the Next.js <Image> src attribute. Next.js proxies images through /_next/image, so a user-controlled URL enables server-side requests to arbitrary internal or external hosts. Validate src against an allowlist of permitted domains.

title: Next.js <Script> src Injection
description: Untrusted user input flows into the Next.js <Script> src attribute, equivalent to injecting an arbitrary <script src> tag. Hardcode the script URL or validate against an allowlist of trusted domains.

title: Dynamic import() with User-Controlled Path
description: Untrusted user input flows into a dynamic import() path. In SSR contexts this enables Remote Code Execution via arbitrary module loading. Never use user-controlled values as module paths; use an explicit allowlist map.

title: AngularJS $sce.trustAsHtml / $compile Injection
description: User input flows into $sce.trustAsHtml(), $sce.trustAs(), or $compile(), disabling AngularJS Strict Contextual Escaping. Use $sanitize or migrate away from AngularJS (EOL December 2021).

title: Angular JIT Runtime Template Compilation
description: Dynamic Angular JIT template compilation is detected with a non-literal template string. The JIT compiler uses the Function constructor internally, so user-controlled template strings enable arbitrary code execution. Use AOT compilation and avoid runtime template compilation.

title: Vue <component :is> Router Injection
description: Router data flows directly into <component :is="...">, allowing an attacker to manipulate the URL to render arbitrary global components. Validate the component name against an explicit allowlist.
bad_examples:
<!-- ❌ unsafe Vue template -->
<template>
  <component :is="$route.query.view" />
</template>
good_examples:
<!-- ✅ safe -->
<script setup lang="ts">
import { useRoute } from 'vue-router';
import Dashboard from './Dashboard.vue';
import Profile from './Profile.vue';
const ALLOWED: Record<string, unknown> = { dashboard: Dashboard, profile: Profile };
const comp = ALLOWED[useRoute().query.view as string] ?? Dashboard;
</script>
<template><component :is="comp" /></template>

title: Vue Runtime Template Compilation CSTI
description: Untrusted user input flows into Vue's runtime template compilation via new Vue({ template }), createApp({ template }), or $app.mount(). The Vue compiler uses Function() internally, making user-controlled template strings equivalent to eval().

title: Vue.compile() with Non-Literal Template
description: Vue.compile() or Vue.extend() is called with a non-literal template string. The Vue compiler uses Function() internally, so user-controlled template expressions execute arbitrary JavaScript. Use only static developer-defined template strings.
good_examples:
// ✅ safe - compile only static literals const Component = Vue.compile('
{{ message }}
');

title: SvelteKit handle() Hook HTML Response XSS
description: Request data flows into an HTML Response from the SvelteKit handle() hook without HTML encoding, allowing XSS via URL fragments or headers. HTML-encode all user-controlled values before embedding in server responses.

title: Sensitive Data in localStorage/sessionStorage
description: Sensitive data such as auth tokens, JWTs, or credentials is stored in localStorage or sessionStorage. Both are accessible via JavaScript and can be exfiltrated by any XSS payload. Store auth tokens in HttpOnly cookies instead.

title: Client-Side Secret in Public Env Variable
description: A client-side environment variable with a sensitive name is referenced. Variables prefixed with NEXT_PUBLIC_, VITE_, or REACT_APP_ are inlined into the production JavaScript bundle and visible to all users. Move secrets to server-side environment variables.

title: React Hook Missing Dependency Array
description: React hook (useEffect, useCallback, useMemo) is called without a dependency array, causing the hook to run on every render, leading to performance degradation or infinite loops.

title: Next.js getServerSideProps Raw ORM Object Leak
description: Raw ORM objects returned from getServerSideProps or getStaticProps can expose sensitive hidden fields (passwords, tokens) in the client HTML payload. Map the object to a safe DTO before returning.

title: redux-persist Without Blacklist Persists Secrets
description: redux-persist is configured without a blacklist. The entire Redux state tree is written to localStorage as plaintext JSON, exposing auth tokens and user data to XSS payloads. Add a blacklist for sensitive reducers.

title: Zustand persist Middleware Exposes Sensitive State
description: Zustand store with persist middleware contains sensitive state fields. The middleware defaults to localStorage, exposing auth data to XSS payloads. Use the partialize option to exclude sensitive fields.

title: Redux DevTools Enabled in Production
description: Redux DevTools are enabled without an environment guard. In production, users with the DevTools extension can inspect the entire Redux state tree including auth tokens and secrets. Wrap with a NODE_ENV === 'development' check.

title: React Query dehydrate() Leaks DB Data to HTML
description: A raw DB result flows into dehydrate(queryClient), serializing the query cache into the HTML payload. Sensitive fields become visible in page source. Prefetch only public data or filter fields before caching.

title: Angular Nested subscribe() Memory Leak
description: Nested subscribe() calls detected inside Angular components, leading to callback hell, race conditions, and memory leaks. Refactor using RxJS operators (switchMap, mergeMap).

title: Angular shareReplay() Without refCount Memory Leak
description: shareReplay() is used without {refCount: true}, causing the operator to never unsubscribe from the source. This retains sensitive API responses in memory after component destruction. Use shareReplay({ bufferSize: 1, refCount: true }) instead.

title: Pinia pinia-plugin-persistedstate Persists Secrets
description: Pinia store uses pinia-plugin-persistedstate with persist:true and contains sensitive state fields. The plugin writes entire store state to localStorage. Use the pick option to include only non-sensitive fields.

title: Nuxt Vuex State Object Singleton Leaks Across Requests
description: Vuex state is defined as a plain object instead of a factory function. In SSR (Nuxt), this creates a singleton shared across all requests, leaking one user's data into another's response. Define state as a function: state: () => ({ ... }).

title: Svelte Store Mutation Inside subscribe() Infinite Loop
description: Mutating a Svelte store inside its own .subscribe() callback is an anti-pattern that triggers infinite loops. Use derived stores instead.

title: SvelteKit Cookie sameSite:'none' CSRF Risk
description: cookies.set() is called with sameSite:'none', disabling SameSite CSRF protection. Use sameSite:'lax' (default) or sameSite:'strict' for authentication cookies.

title: SvelteKit Cookie secure:false Allows HTTP Transmission
description: cookies.set() is called with secure:false, allowing the cookie to be transmitted over unencrypted HTTP and enabling interception. Use secure:true to restrict transmission to HTTPS only.

title: DOM Open Redirect via window.location
description: Untrusted user input controls page navigation via window.location or history.pushState. An attacker can redirect victims to a malicious site. Validate the URL is a relative path or matches an explicit allowlist.

title: Host Header SSRF in Server-Side Code
description: The HTTP Host or X-Forwarded-Host header is used to construct backend requests. Attackers can spoof this header to route server-side traffic to internal hosts. Hardcode the API domain instead of trusting request headers.

title: Open Redirect via ?redirect= Parameter
description: A redirect-related URL parameter (?next=, ?redirect=, ?url=, etc.) flows into a navigation function without validation. Validate the value is a relative path or matches an allowlist.

title: Next.js redirect() Open Redirect
description: Untrusted user input flows into Next.js redirect() or permanentRedirect(), which emit HTTP 307/308 responses controlling the redirect destination. Validate against a relative-path constraint or domain allowlist.

title: Angular Router navigateByUrl Open Redirect
description: Untrusted data flows into Angular navigateByUrl() or navigate(). An attacker can redirect users to an external malicious site. Validate the target against an allowlist or restrict to safe local paths.

title: Angular Route Params to window.location Open Redirect
description: Angular route params flow directly into window.location assignment, bypassing Angular Router's built-in protection. Use Angular Router's navigate() and validate the target path.

title: Vue Router push() Open Redirect
description: Untrusted data flows into Vue Router push() or replace(). An attacker can redirect users to external malicious sites. Validate the URL is a relative path before navigating.

title: Nuxt Server-Side SSRF via User-Controlled URL
description: Untrusted user input flows into a Nuxt backend API call or server route fetch. An attacker can force the server to request arbitrary internal or external hosts. Validate the URL against a domain allowlist or restrict to relative paths.

title: Nuxt navigateTo() Open Redirect
description: Untrusted user input flows into Nuxt navigateTo(). Paths prefixed with '//' redirect to external domains, and whitespace-injected javascript: URIs can bypass protocol checks. Validate URL is a relative path or restrict protocol to http/https.

title: SvelteKit redirect() Open Redirect
description: Untrusted user input flows into SvelteKit's redirect(). SvelteKit performs no origin or protocol validation on the redirect URL, allowing javascript: URIs and external domain redirects. Validate the URL is a relative path or matches a trusted origin.

title: SvelteKit event.url Host Header SSRF
description: SvelteKit event.url is constructed from the incoming Host header without validation. An attacker-controlled Host header causes server-side fetch() to contact arbitrary hosts. Use the ORIGIN environment variable or a hardcoded base URL instead of event.url.

title: Prototype Pollution via Dynamic Property Assignment
description: Untrusted data is used to dynamically assign object properties. An attacker can inject '__proto__' to manipulate the global Object prototype, enabling XSS, RCE, or logic bypass. Avoid dynamic property keys from user input.

title: Prototype Pollution via Recursive Merge
description: Untrusted data flows into a recursive merge function. If the input contains '__proto__', it pollutes the global Object prototype. Use safe merge libraries or validate the input before merging.

title: Prototype Pollution via Object.setPrototypeOf()
description: Untrusted data flows into Object.setPrototypeOf() or Reflect.setPrototypeOf(), directly replacing the prototype chain of the target object. Never pass user-controlled data as the prototype argument.

title: Prototype Pollution via Reflect.set with User Key
description: Untrusted data is used as a property key in Reflect.set() or Reflect.defineProperty(). An attacker can use '__proto__' as the key to pollute Object.prototype. Validate or blocklist '__proto__', 'constructor', and 'prototype' before use.

title: Prototype Pollution via jQuery $.extend(true, ...)
description: Untrusted data flows into jQuery $.extend(true, ...) deep merge. If the input contains '__proto__', it pollutes Object.prototype across the application. Upgrade jQuery to >= 3.4.0 and sanitize inputs before merging.

title: Arbitrary window[] Access via User-Controlled Key
description: Untrusted input is used to access window[] or globalThis[] via bracket notation, allowing reading or invoking any global (eval, Function, document.cookie, etc.). Constrain allowed keys to an explicit allowlist.

title: window.name Attacker-Controlled XSS Source
description: window.name is used in a security-sensitive operation. window.name persists across cross-origin navigations and is fully controlled by any previously visited page. Never use window.name as a trusted data source.

title: Sensitive Data Assigned to window.*
description: A property with a sensitive name is assigned to window or globalThis. All scripts in the browsing context including third-party scripts and extensions can read window.* properties. Use module-scoped variables or closures instead.

title: Lodash _.template() Code Execution
description: Lodash _.template() with the 'variable' option allows arbitrary code execution when template strings are user-controlled. Never generate templates dynamically from user input.

title: Markdown Parser XSS via Unsanitized Output
description: Untrusted data flows into a Markdown parser. Most parsers (marked, showdown, markdown-it) do not sanitize HTML tags or 'javascript:' URIs by default. Wrap the output with DOMPurify.sanitize() before inserting into the DOM.

title: DOMPurify Dangerous Configuration Bypasses XSS Protection
description: DOMPurify is configured with options that disable core protections such as allowing 'script'/'iframe' tags, event handler attributes, or unknown protocols. Remove dangerous config options to restore sanitization.

title: sanitize-html Dangerous Config Bypasses XSS Protection
description: sanitize-html is configured with allowedTags or allowedAttributes that permit script execution, including 'script'/'iframe' tags or event handler attributes. Use the default config or a minimal safe subset.

title: Handlebars Template Injection RCE
description: Untrusted user input flows into Handlebars template compilation. A user-controlled template string allows executing arbitrary code. Templates must be static developer-defined strings, never user input.

title: Nunjucks SSTI / RCE via renderString()
description: Untrusted user input flows into nunjucks.renderString() as the template string. Template expressions execute in the Nunjucks context and can access Node.js internals. Pass user input only as data, never as the template string itself.

title: Eta SSTI / RCE via renderString()
description: Untrusted user input flows into Eta's renderString(). User-controlled template arguments allow arbitrary code injection via Eta's template syntax. Template strings must be static developer-defined literals.

title: superjson Deserialization Prototype Pollution / RCE
description: Untrusted data flows into superjson.deserialize() or superjson.parse(). Attacker-controlled input can trigger prototype pollution leading to RCE. Validate all input with a schema (e.g., Zod) before deserializing.

title: devalue Deserialization Prototype Pollution / RCE
description: Untrusted data flows into devalue.parse() or devalue.unflatten(). Attacker-controlled input can cause prototype pollution leading to RCE. Validate incoming data with a schema before passing to devalue.

title: jsPDF XSS via User-Controlled HTML Input
description: Untrusted user input flows into a jsPDF method that accepts HTML or executes code. Multiple attack surfaces exist including HTML injection and script injection. Sanitize all user input with DOMPurify before passing to jsPDF.

title: html2pdf.js XSS via innerHTML Assignment
description: Untrusted user input is passed as a string to html2pdf(). When the source is a string, html2pdf.js assigns it directly to innerHTML. Sanitize with DOMPurify before passing, or pass a pre-rendered DOM element.

title: JWT Decoded Without Signature Verification
description: jwt.decode() is used without signature verification. jwt.decode() only base64-decodes the payload and performs no cryptographic validation. An attacker can forge arbitrary claims. Always use jwt.verify() with an explicit algorithm list.

title: D3.js .html() XSS via User-Controlled Content
description: Untrusted user input flows into D3.js's .html() method or a dangerous attribute binding. D3's .html() sets innerHTML directly and is an intentional XSS sink. Use .text() for user-controlled content or sanitize with DOMPurify before calling .html().

title: Highcharts useHTML:true XSS via User Data
description: Untrusted data flows into a Highcharts chart option with useHTML:true. In this mode, Highcharts inserts string values via innerHTML. Sanitize user input with DOMPurify before passing to chart configuration.
